description_long: This object is a fragment of a white-painted brick wall, featuring a rough, irregular outline as if it has been broken or chipped away from a larger structure. The surface displays a classic running bond brickwork pattern, with each brick slightly textured and the mortar lines clearly visible. The paint appears matte and evenly applied, giving the wall a clean but slightly aged appearance. The edges are jagged and uneven, suggesting damage or intentional removal. The wall section is relatively thin, as seen from the side views, and does not contain any visible openings or decorative elements. Its overall look is utilitarian, typical of urban or industrial environments.
description: A white-painted, irregularly broken fragment of brick wall with a classic running bond pattern and rough, jagged edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the white brick wall with visible mortar lines and rough, broken edges.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thinness of the wall fragment and the uneven, chipped profile.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the brick pattern and jagged outline.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the wall's thinness and irregular edge.
